meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By integrating a dynamic pricing engine early, you force yourself to define business logic, value propositions, and user acquisition flows. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to implement Stripe webhooks, effectively turning code tutorials into revenue-generating assets.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks watching video tutorials, copying boilerplate code, and feeling like you are making progress. Yet, your production dashboard remains empty. The 'tutorial trap' is a silent killer for founders; it gives you the illusion of productivity while delaying the only metric that matters: your first dollar.

Today, we break the cycle. We are pivoting from passive learning to aggressive building by focusing on the most critical SaaS component—pricing. By architecting a pricing module, you stop building features and start building a business. Let’s leverage AI-driven tooling to move from empty repos to a live, monetizable SaaS product in hours.

steps.title: Define Value-Based Tiers
steps.description: Don't guess at pricing. Define three clear tiers: Starter, Pro, and Enterprise, mapped to specific user outcomes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema for a B2B AI tool. Include fields for tier name, monthly cost, feature set array, and usage limits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing_config.json file based on the schema generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Logic
steps.description: Build the backend logic that gates features based on the user's active subscription tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a middleware function for a Node.js/Express app that checks user subscription status against a required feature flag.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the feature-gating middleware in the auth controller and add unit tests for each tier.'

steps.title: Stripe Integration Scaffolding
steps.description: Connect your application to Stripe to handle checkout sessions and customer portal redirection.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Stripe checkout session handler in TypeScript that maps to our defined pricing tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a /api/checkout route that accepts a price_id and redirects the user to the Stripe hosted page.'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Ensure your database updates when payments succeed or fail via Stripe webhooks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure webhook handler for Stripe events: customer.subscription.created and customer.subscription.deleted.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the webhook listener in a new file, ensuring signature verification and database sync.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Push your changes to a staging environment and test the end-to-end payment flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel that includes environment variables for Stripe public and secret keys.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure the Vercel project and deploy the current branch to preview environment.'

pro_tips: Never hardcode prices; always use Stripe Price IDs fetched from your database to allow for dynamic price changes without redeploying code.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your middleware to avoid churn during failed card updates.
Use a local tunnel tool like ngrok with Claude Code to test webhooks on your local machine instantly.
Prioritize building the 'Cancel Subscription' flow before the 'Subscribe' flow; it builds user trust and reduces chargebacks.
Always log Stripe event IDs in your database to prevent duplicate processing of the same payment event.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, shift your focus to analytics. Install a lightweight tracking library to measure which features are most clicked, allowing you to iterate on your pricing tiers based on actual user behavior.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of earning. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap. The only thing missing is your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
